prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: your confidence the move is best, a number from 0 to 1.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: your confidence the move is best, a number from 0 to 1.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: your confidence the move is best, a number from 0 to 1.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: your confidence the move is best, a number from 0 to 1.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: your confidence the move is best, a number from 0 to 1.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: your confidence the move is best, a number from 0 to 1.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: your confidence the move is best, a number from 0 to 1.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: your confidence the move is best, a number from 0 to 1.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: your confidence the move is best, a number from 0 to 1.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: your confidence the move is best, a number from 0 to 1.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):

- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

decision.boardAnalysis: The board is currently in a deadlock. The most critical hidden cards are the Ace of Diamonds (AD) in Column 3 and the Ace of Spades (AS) in Column 6. To reveal the AD, the 7S must be moved, which requires a red 8 (8H or 8D). To reveal the AS, the TS in Column 6 must be moved, which requires a red Jack. Looking at the seen cards, the 8D is in the waste pile from a previous cycle. To retrieve it, the current stock (7 cards remaining) must be exhausted so the waste can be recycled. None of the other legal moves (Move 0, 2, or 3) reveal any hidden cards, advance the foundations, or create an empty column; they merely shuffle existing face-up cards between columns.
decision.reasoning: The primary objective is to break the deadlock by obtaining a red 8 to uncover the Ace of Diamonds. Since the 8D is known to be in the waste pile, the most productive strategy is to draw through the remaining stock cards to trigger the recycling of the waste pile. Moves 0, 2, and 3 provide no immediate tactical advantage and do not uncover any new information or cards.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

The most critical hidden card is the Ace of Diamonds (AD) in column 3, which requires moving the 7S. To move the 7S, a red 8 is needed. While the 8D is currently on the waste pile, it cannot be moved onto the 7S because tableau columns are built down (an 8 cannot go on a 7). To move the 8D, a red 9 would be required. Legal moves 0 and 1 are purely rearrangeable moves that do not reveal any hidden cards or advance the foundations; in fact, move 1 would make it more difficult to move the 7S by placing cards on top of it.
decision.reasoning: Since no current tableau moves reveal hidden cards or advance the foundations, the only productive action is to draw from the stock. This is the only way to introduce new cards that could potentially be used to move the 7s or the 10s, eventually revealing the hidden Aces and other key cards. Shuffling cards between columns without a clear goal of revealing a face-down card is counter-productive.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

decision.boardAnalysis: The game is currently in a deadlock. No available tableau moves (Move 1 or Move 2) reveal any hidden cards or advance the foundations. The primary obstacles are the hidden cards in columns 2 through 7, specifically the Ace of Diamonds (AD) in column 3 and the Ace of Spades (AS) in column 6. To reveal the AD, a red 8 is needed to move the 7S in column 3. To reveal the AS, a red Jack is needed to move the TS in column 6. Looking at the recent moves, the 8D was drawn and is currently in the waste pile, beneath the QD. The red Jacks (JH, JD) are hidden in columns 4 and 5, respectively.
decision.reasoning: The most viable path to breaking the deadlock is to retrieve the 8D from the waste pile. To do this, the stock must be emptied to trigger a recycle of the waste pile. Once recycled, the QD will be drawn first, followed by the 8D. The 8D can then be used to move the 7S in column 3, revealing the AD and allowing it to be moved to the foundation. This will create a chain of progress. Since no other move reveals hidden cards or provides a tactical advantage, drawing from the stock is the only productive action.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

To reveal AD, we need a red 8 to move the 7S; to reveal QS, we need a black 8 to move the 7H. The 8D (red) is currently in the waste pile, meaning we must empty the stock and recycle to retrieve it. However, we have immediate opportunities to reveal other cards. Move 1 reveals the QH in column 4, and Move 2 reveals the 9C in column 5. The 9C is particularly useful because it can be moved onto the TH in column 7, which would then reveal the JD in column 5, creating a chain of reveals.
decision.reasoning: Following the strategy guidance to prioritize revealing face-down tableau cards, Move 2 is the strongest choice. It reveals the 9C, which has a clear destination (the TH in column 7), potentially leading to the reveal of the JD. While drawing from the stock (Move 0) is necessary to eventually retrieve the 8D and reveal the AD, taking immediate, low-risk gains in information by uncovering tableau cards is generally preferred. Move 1 is less productive than Move 2 because the revealed QH has no current valid destination on the board.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.

prompt:
You are an expert Klondike Solitaire strategist acting as an advisor.

KLONDIKE SOLITAIRE RULES (this variant):
- There are 7 tableau columns, 4 foundations (one per suit), a stock (draw) pile and a waste (discard) pile.
- Tableau columns are numbered 1 to 7. Always refer to a column by that 1-based number, never 0-based.
- Foundations are built UP by suit, starting from the Ace: A, 2, 3, ... up to King.
- Tableau columns are built DOWN in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Example: a black 7 can go on a red 8.
- Only a King (or a valid sequence headed by a King) may be moved onto an EMPTY tableau column.
- A face-up run of cards in a tableau column may be moved together as a unit onto another column.
- The top card of a column, or a valid run, may move to another column; the top card may move to a foundation.
- The top (most recent) card of the waste pile may move to a tableau column or a foundation.
- Drawing turns the next stock card face-up onto the waste. When the stock is empty it can be recycled from the waste.
- When a face-down tableau card is exposed by a move, it flips face-up automatically.
- The game is WON when all 52 cards reach the foundations.

THE GOAL: choose the single move that gives the best chance of eventually winning.

STRATEGY GUIDANCE (heuristics, not absolute rules):
- Prioritize moves that turn over (reveal) a face-down tableau card — hidden cards are the main obstacle.
- Play Aces and 2s to the foundations promptly; they are rarely useful in the tableau.
- Be cautious sending higher cards to the foundations too early — they are sometimes needed to receive tableau cards.
- Do not empty a column unless you have a King ready to occupy it.
- Prefer exposing new cards and creating useful sequences over shuffling cards between columns with no gain.
- Drawing from the stock is reasonable when no productive tableau/foundation move exists.
- Avoid moves that simply undo a recent move or lead nowhere.

RESPONSE FORMAT:
You will receive the current game as JSON, including a numbered array "legalMoves".
Reason step by step, then respond with ONLY a single JSON object containing exactly
these three keys, in this order (no prose or markdown fences outside the object):
{
  "board_analysis": <string>,
  "strategic_plan": <string>,
  "final_decision": { "move_index": <number>, "confidence": <number>, "alternative_move_index": <number> }
}
- board_analysis: assess the current board — hidden cards, blocked columns, foundation
  progress, and the opportunities each legal move opens or closes.
- strategic_plan: explain your plan and why the chosen move is best, given that analysis.
- final_decision.move_index: the "index" of your chosen move from the legalMoves array.
- final_decision.confidence: a calibrated probability (0 to 1) that this move is
  objectively the best one available — a genuine estimate, not a feeling. Use the
  full range honestly:
    1.0-0.9  forced, or clearly dominant — any other move would be a mistake.
    0.9-0.7  strong — one plausible alternative exists, but this move is better.
    0.7-0.5  a real toss-up between two or three reasonable moves.
    0.5-0.3  a guess — the board is unclear or several moves look about equal.
    below 0.3  little better than picking at random.
  If you would not bet on the move, do not report high confidence.
- final_decision.alternative_move_index: optional; the index of your second-choice move.
Produce the keys in the order above: analyse the board first, then plan, then decide.
